purpose: Balanced regressions separating exact measurements, target identity, causal attribution, physical continuity, historical inference, and transport.

fixtures.source_question: Did allele A increase from 0.20 to 0.45 over ten generations in this sampled population?
required: answer from the population boundary assay timepoints sampling uncertainty and missingness
preserve the measured change without imposing selection drift or adaptation
state the exact population and time scope
forbidden: require a drift budget merely to report the measured change
infer selection from the direction of change
erase the observation because the generator is unknown

fixtures.condition: A study defines allele A, an environmental regime, and a frequency increase, then attributes the change to selection without testing drift, migration, transmission distortion, linkage, or demographic change.
required: preserve the frequency trajectory
keep target identity separate from failed causal attribution
reject the selection conclusion because necessary alternatives remain open
forbidden: classify the frequency measurement as identity failure
soften the failed claim into may have contributed
treat frequency change as the selection process

fixtures.condition: Independent replicate lineages begin from a characterized population. A resource regime is manipulated, descendant output is measured, drift and contamination are bounded, and the direction reverses when the regime reverses.
required: admit exact regime to descendant-output causality when the intervention burden closes
preserve open internal molecular segments without denying the effect
keep natural-population transport separate
forbidden: reject the effect because the internal route is partial
generalize to universal fitness or adaptation
require a clonal ancestor when the starting population is characterized

fixtures.condition: Replicate neutral markers are propagated through controlled finite bottlenecks with no measured fitness differences or migration, and variance matches a preregistered finite-sampling model across independent lineages.
required: permit exact drift causality at the tested population and transmission scope
bind the claim to the bottleneck model and marker conditions
report uncertainty and model deviations
forbidden: force the result into adaptation
treat drift as unexplained randomness
transport the model to unrelated populations

fixtures.condition: A selection test is nonsignificant and the authors conclude the trait is neutral without an equivalence margin, power analysis, model comparison, or positive drift discrimination.
required: preserve the estimate and uncertainty
reject neutrality as a conclusion from failure to reject selection
require positive model discrimination appropriate to the claim
forbidden: translate nonsignificance into no selection
treat absence of detection as evidence of neutrality
erase the measured estimate

fixtures.condition: A paper reports invasion fitness defined by an explicit dynamical model, resident state, mutant type, environment, timescale, and uncertainty.
required: permit fitness as bounded shorthand for the recoverable quantity
retain the model conditions and uncertainty
keep it separate from other fitness generators and historical adaptation
forbidden: reject fitness because the word appears on a cue list
treat it as an intrinsic property outside the model
merge invasion fitness with lifetime reproductive success

fixtures.source_question: Why did this allele spread? Because it had higher fitness.
required: state that higher fitness supplies neither a defined quantity nor a physical generator
identify the missing type environment time population comparator and measurement
preserve any separately supplied frequency observation
forbidden: accept fitness as an intrinsic cause
replace it with survival of the fittest
invent a selective regime

fixtures.condition: A controlled perturbation establishes that trait T changes current output Y, and the same result is used to claim T originally evolved for Y.
required: admit the exact current perturbation relation when its burden closes
reject transfer from current role to historical origin
preserve open regime phylogeny and alternative-route burdens
forbidden: erase the current effect because origin fails
use current usefulness as historical evidence
call present dependency the selected effect

fixtures.condition: A phenotype-level historical claim has reproducible heritable variation, independent evidence of the past regime, temporal ordering, comparative support, replicated differential-persistence predictions, and tests against drift, correlated selection, constraint, and exaptation.
required: permit causal admission when all applicable historical burdens close
keep the claim at the supported lineage trait regime and time resolution
avoid requiring an exact nucleotide for a phenotype-level claim
forbidden: reject all historical inference categorically
require a molecular path beyond the claimed resolution
promote the result to optimal design

fixtures.condition: A sequence is conserved under a stated alignment and sampling method, and the paper concludes it is important, functional, and adaptive.
required: preserve the conservation relation
separate current function purifying selection and adaptation
require direct dependency evidence for function and comparative discrimination for selection
forbidden: erase conservation
allow conservation to supply importance or adaptation
compress all targets into one verdict

fixtures.condition: Two species show a similar structure under a stated measurement, and similarity is used as proof of homology and common selective origin.
required: preserve similarity
require correspondence reconstruction and applicable discordance tests for homology
keep homology and shared selective regime separate
forbidden: treat similarity as common ancestry or shared function
erase the similarity observation
require irrelevant discordance audits mechanically

fixtures.condition: A homology claim specifies characters, developmental and positional correspondence, phylogenetic reconstruction with uncertainty, alternative histories, and applicable convergence, reversal, introgression, or lineage-sorting tests.
required: admit homology at the stated character lineage model and uncertainty scope
preserve alternative histories and discordance limits
keep shared function and selective regime separate
forbidden: require optimality for homology
translate homology into identical mechanism
erase uncertainty because one topology is preferred

fixtures.condition: Replicated experimental populations begin with characterized standing variation, undergo a controlled regime, and show repeatable haplotype and phenotype shifts with bounded contamination and drift.
required: admit the exact regime-specific relation when intervention and population burdens close
extract starting frequencies linkage recombination and replicate-lineage structure
avoid imposing a new-mutation supply calculation
forbidden: require a locked clonal ancestor
require a mutation-supply calculation for standing variation
transport the result to natural populations automatically

fixtures.condition: A controlled laboratory regime produces repeatable adaptation, and the result is claimed to establish the same effect and magnitude in wild populations.
required: preserve the laboratory result
separate environment demography population structure timescale and regime transport
require direct transport evidence before transferring magnitude
forbidden: erase the lab result because transport is open
treat ecological plausibility as transport evidence
transfer the laboratory coefficient unchanged

fixtures.condition: A variant is associated with a complex behavior with a reported effect distribution and heterogeneity, then described as a gene for the behavior.
required: preserve association population environment effect distribution and uncertainty
reject the residual purpose or essence supplied by gene-for phrasing
keep prediction mechanism organism effect and historical maintenance separate
forbidden: erase the association
treat association as mechanism
infer a unitary behavioral object from gene-for language

fixtures.condition: A controlled edit changes a specified binding interaction, rescue restores it, downstream molecular output changes with timing and specificity, and no historical function claim is made.
required: admit the exact edited molecular relation when manipulation rescue timing and specificity close
preserve open organism-level and historical routes
avoid calling partial downstream mechanics a complete mechanism
forbidden: require phylogenetic reconstruction for the molecular intervention effect
promote the effect to historical function
reject exact causality because broader meaning is open

fixtures.condition: A model defines an objective, feasible set, physical and developmental constraints, uncertainty, and a local optimum tested against alternative objectives and constraints.
required: admit the model-defined local optimum at its model and feasible-set scope
report sensitivity to objective and constraint choices
keep optimality separate from adaptation history and global perfection
forbidden: reject all optimality language categorically
call a local optimum best possible globally
use the optimum as proof of historical selection

fixtures.condition: A comparative study treats 200 species as independent observations despite one shared phylogeny and reports a precise trait-environment association.
required: model shared ancestry or justify independence
qualify information precision and transport by phylogenetic dependence
preserve measured species data while limiting causal claims
forbidden: use nominal species count as independent sample size
treat phylogenetic correction as automatic causal closure
erase data because independence is imperfect

fixtures.condition: Thousands of measurements across generations come from one experimental lineage and are treated as thousands of independent evolutionary replicates.
required: treat replicate lineage or population as the relevant independent unit
preserve the within-lineage trajectory
reject precision and replication claims based on repeated measurements alone
forbidden: count generations or assays as independent populations
erase the time series
call one lineage independent replication

fixtures.condition: Two lineages reach a similar phenotype through different measured genetic and developmental routes, and the shared phenotype is used to assert one homologous mechanism and one adaptation.
required: preserve convergent output while separating route identity and history
treat route divergence as premise-breaking for one shared mechanism
require lineage-specific historical audits
forbidden: use the same phenotype to supply homology or common origin
erase convergence because the shared story fails
compress distinct routes into one target
